subject: Cloud Computing
title: Multi-Cloud Strategy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Multi-Cloud Strategy.

hook.text: What if your cloud provider has a massive outage? If everything runs on AWS, everything goes down.

buildup.text: Multi-cloud means using two or more providers: maybe AWS for compute and Google for AI services.

discovery.text: It reduces vendor lock-in and improves resilience. If one provider fails, others keep running.

twist.text: But multi-cloud is hard. Each provider has different APIs, pricing, and behavior. Complexity doubles.

climax.text: Most companies that claim multi-cloud actually use one primary provider and one for a few niche tasks.

punchline.text: Multi-cloud sounds smart. Making it work is the hard part.

quiz.question: What is the main challenge of a multi-cloud strategy?
options: Each provider has different APIs and behaviors, greatly increasing complexity
Cloud providers refuse to work alongside competitors
Multi-cloud always costs exactly twice as much as single-cloud
